note: This file documents competitor patterns observed through EX-STL leads who switched to Stayful. Do not use this to criticise competitors directly in conversations. Use it to understand and address specific failure patterns.

competitors.name: Pass the Keys
observed_failure_patterns: Income projections significantly above delivery (£1,500-2,000 promised, ~£1,000 delivered in entry 036)
Income inconsistency blamed on market rather than management
General credibility concern when projections underperform
competitors.close_approach_when_mentioned: Acknowledge the income gap. Explain Stayful projections are built from actual comparable data, not winning numbers. Show how review maturity and listing quality drive real income.

competitors.name: MyProperty Host
observed_failure_patterns: Damage not reported to owner in time for insurance claim
Account manager left without handover — service continuity failure
competitors.close_approach_when_mentioned: Name the specific failure: damage reporting SLA, account manager continuity, check-in reliability. Show Stayful's systematic checkout process and named property manager model.

competitors.name: Clifton Valley Lets
observed_failure_patterns: Credibility concern from owner about new manager
competitors.close_approach_when_mentioned: Address the reviews question directly: Stayful doesn't lose reviews on switch if the listing is migrated correctly. Named accountability is the core differentiator.

competitors.name: Hello Guest
observed_failure_patterns: Only 3 reviews in 5+ months of trading
6-month contract term created anxiety about switching again
competitors.close_approach_when_mentioned: Frame Stayful's initial 6-month term as a structured performance period, not a lock-in. 3 reviews in 5 months is a listing and pricing problem — explain what causes this and how Stayful avoids it.

competitors.name: Residential Estates/Guests
observed_failure_patterns: Pooled listing: lead's photos used to promote whole building
£1,600 toilet leak — maintenance failure not caught in check-out
4G WiFi caused lost Amazon relocation client and negative review
competitors.close_approach_when_mentioned: Individual listing identity is the direct counter. Systematic maintenance check-out is the direct counter. Transparent fee model (no software charge) is the direct counter.

competitors.name: Nice Living
observed_failure_patterns: Company winding down due to double council tax penalty exposure
competitors.close_approach_when_mentioned: Not a quality failure — a regulatory/business viability failure. Opportunity: portfolio migration. Stayful's stability and track record are the close.

general_competitor_objection_handling.rule: Never criticise competitors by name in conversation. Describe Stayful's process instead.
general_competitor_objection_handling.template: I won't speak to how [provider] runs their business. What I can tell you is exactly how we handle [the specific thing that went wrong].
comparison_questions_to_give_leads: Do I get my own individual listing or am I pooled with other properties?
What is your damage reporting SLA from checkout?
Who is my named contact and what happens if they leave?
What are all the fees — including software charges, photography, onboarding?
What happens in your worst month for a property like mine?
